parsed_review.summary: CP00-621 is a descriptor-only Data Room/VDR pack (RP20.P04.M04.S01-RP20.P05.M00.S10, risk C, 150 units) bridging the P04 UI secondary-workflow/permission-audit/fixture/test/Hermes/Claude/handoff descriptor tail into the P05 fixture-foundation head. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the implementation is complete and internally consistent across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the contract JSON projections, the validator script, model.test.js, and the README CP00-621 entry. The CP621 binding, section rows, micro-titles, and deliverable distribution match the baseline manifest and prompt exactly; I independently re-derived all 150 row classifications and every deliverable count ties out (ui 59, implementation 39, claude_review 14, security_audit 14, fixture 12, test 6, hermes_evidence 6) and the 8 section row-counts tie out (22x5 + 20 + 10 + 10 = 150), so coverage validation is substantive rather than vacuous — the freezeCp621Result base populates every field the validator asserts. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in depth: the freeze chain sets all dispatches_*/executes_*/writes_*/reads_* guards false, the CP621 validator asserts ~45 such conditions including the new ui_state_snapshot/unauthorized_count_query/p05_fixture_foundation/golden_case/cross_tenant_fixture runtime guards, and the no_write_attestation is mirrored field-for-field in the contract JSON and asserted by tests. Authority boundary is preserved everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes is used only for the deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, descriptor-only receipt_shape) and is not embedded in product runtime. The pack makes no overclaim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, and the closeout artifacts (command-evidence, claude-review-result, construction-inspection, adjudication) all honestly report pending; the changed-file-scope correctly isolates the active pack files from unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, etc.). Review-hardening preconditions hold: readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON), prompt 2397 bytes within limit, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, schema present, summary_only_review_allowed=false for this Risk C pack; no pre-existing raw-output/receipt exists for CP00-621 to reject. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One P3 informational note: production_ready closeout remains gated on recorded passing validation-command evidence, the Hermes H20 gate, adjudication finalization, and human final approval — process steps outside this read-only review's authority, not code defects. This review is independent and read-only; it is not final approval and does not assert enterprise trust.

findings.title: Production-ready closeout still gated on un-recorded validation runs, Hermes gate, and human approval
findings.details: command-evidence.json reports status=pending with an empty commands array (line 9), no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, and hermes_gate=pending (command_ids rp00_control_plane_contract_validate, spec_requirement_validate); gate_outcome=pending_pack_evidence_creation. claude-review-result.json (exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false, valid_review_receipts=[]), construction-inspection.json (verdict=PENDING_REVIEW), and adjudication.md are likewise pending; manifest.json reports production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false. The CP621 descriptor implementation, contract projections, tests, validator script, and no-write/no-real-data + authority boundaries are complete and internally consistent (verified by read-only inspection plus independent deliverable-count math totalling 150). A read-only review can confirm the code is structured to satisfy the expected validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, etc.) but cannot confirm those commands were actually executed and passed, because no command evidence is recorded yet.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready to true, record passing evidence for the listed validation commands in command-evidence.json, complete the Hermes H20 gate, finalize adjudication and construction inspection, and obtain human final approval. Keep the pack descriptor-only with runtime_ready=false until a downstream pack (CP00-622, RP20.P05.M01.S01 onward) explicitly opens runtime behavior under human sign-off; do not weaken the production_ready gate on the basis of this read-only review.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected pre-closeout process state, not a code defect. Does not block the review verdict (PASS_WITH_FINDINGS) and does not block pack closeout from the reviewer's side; it documents the residual mechanical/human gates the closeout tooling must still enforce.
